title: Talk:2024 Israeli military operation in the West Bank
wikitext:
{{Contentious topics/talk notice|topic=a-i}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=start| {{WikiProject Israel|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Palestine|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Israel Palestine Collaboration}} {{WikiProject Military history|Middle-Eastern=y|Post-Cold-War=y|b1=no|b2=no|b3=no|b4=yes|b5=yes|class=Start}} }} {{old move|date1=1 September 2024|name1=2024 Israeli military operation in the northern West Bank|destination1=2024 Israeli military operation in the West Bank|result1=moved|link1=Special:Permalink/1244213915#Requested move 1 September 2024|date2=24 September 2024|destination2=Operation Summer Camps|result2=no consensus|link2=Special:Permalink/1251956599#Requested move 24 September 2024}} == Lede == {{ping|Galamore}} why did you remove the perpetrator of the occupation in the opening paragraph? And why did you add “counterterrorism” POV to it? And why did you add an entire POV paragraph sourced to Israeli websites? [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 20:00, 29 August 2024 (UTC) :Since when do we discriminate against reliable sources based on their country of origin? Should we stop using Al Jazeera as well? And by the way, counterterrorism label seems to be supported by multiple reliable sources. [[User:ABHammad|ABHammad]] ([[User talk:ABHammad|talk]]) 06:44, 30 August 2024 (UTC) ::Since the existence of the conflict of interest principle. Calling ToI, Ynet and JP, which rely extensively and almost exclusively on Israeli military statements, are not to be considered reliable sources by any reasonable standard. [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 08:49, 30 August 2024 (UTC) :::It looks like someone invented a new rule. If these ssources undergo rigorous fact-checking they should not be judged solely on their country of origin. Meanwhile, I see a liberal use of Al Jazeera, which, according to our policy, many on Wikipedia recognize as a biased source on the Arab-Israeli conflict. [[User:ABHammad|ABHammad]] ([[User talk:ABHammad|talk]]) 04:33, 31 August 2024 (UTC) ::::AJ is green at RSN and was snow closed in a recent RFC as reliable for the IP conflict so its reliability is not in question. AJ is also not a "side" in the conflict, which Israel is, so it would be better if there were at least one non Israeli source, do you have one? [[User:Selfstudier|Selfstudier]] ([[User talk:Selfstudier|talk]]) 09:17, 31 August 2024 (UTC) ::::It is biased but reliable, and in the case of this war (with focus on Gaza) it is the only source to be used given the lack of other outlet’s boots on the ground within the region [[User:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|The Great Mule of Eupatoria]] ([[User talk:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|talk]]) 09:23, 31 August 2024 (UTC) ::::"Rigorous fact checking" like when JP claimed a dead Palestinian girl was a doll, or when ToI laundered for months the Hamas HQ below Al-Shifa lie. No one is judging them on their country of origin, a classic accusation to stifle any criticism of Israel; Haaretz is considered a reliable source on WP and I would not object to it being referenced here. AJ is reliable per WP. So please, do not make any further false claims beyond these two just debunked ones. [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 10:14, 31 August 2024 (UTC) == Terror == The PIJ named their operation Horror of the Camps رعب المخيمات, not Terror of the Camps; Al Mayadeen, and particularly Ro'ya's lousy churnalism, are not reliable sources in any aspect, whether related or unrelated to the conflict. [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 10:34, 30 August 2024 (UTC) :Not direct translations but both can work, رعب is horror, رهب is terror [[User:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|The Great Mule of Eupatoria]] ([[User talk:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|talk]]) 05:27, 31 August 2024 (UTC) == "The Israeli operation came about following a failed suicide bombing in Tel Aviv by a Hamas operative, and calls by Hamas for the renewel of suicide attacks." == "Renewel", seriously? If you're going to write propaganda, at least spell it right. [[User:21fafs|21fafs]] ([[User talk:21fafs|talk]]) 16:44, 30 August 2024 (UTC) :Fixed the spelling and tagged for possible synth, however please properly present edit requests in future and avoid making speeches. [[User:Selfstudier|Selfstudier]] ([[User talk:Selfstudier|talk]]) 17:42, 30 August 2024 (UTC) == Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 30 August 2024 == {{edit extended-protected|2024 Israeli military operation in the northern West Bank|answered=yes}} '''"Terror of the Camps"''' to '''"Horror of the Camps"''' per [[WP:ALMAYADEEN]] and [[WP:TERRORIST]]. Another argument for "Horror" is that it is the classical translation of «رعب» and my reference for that is the Arabic version Wikipedia articles titles that contain "Horror" in their english version. "Terror" is usually the classical translation for «إرهاب» — '''[[User:The_Cheesedealer|🧀Cheesedealer]] ''[[User_talk:The_Cheesedealer|!!!⚟]]''''' 17:52, 30 August 2024 (UTC) :{{done}} It has been done already, so I'll just mark this done. [[User:Ilovefood123123|Ilovefood123123]] ([[User talk:Ilovefood123123|talk]]) 10:18, 31 August 2024 (UTC) == Current event tag == Self-explanatory but for the sake of completeness: this is an ongoing conflict of total duration so far <48h. The tag really should have been placed there from the outset. [[User:AllPurposeScientist|'''AllPurposeScientist''']][[User_talk:AllPurposeScientist|blah]] 17:58, 30 August 2024 (UTC) :{{Re|AllPurposeScientist}} {{tq| The tag really should have been placed there from the outset}} It was and was subsequently removed because not many editors are editing/commenting, still the case so Idk why you have restored it. [[User:Selfstudier|Selfstudier]] ([[User talk:Selfstudier|talk]]) 18:03, 30 August 2024 (UTC) ::My understanding of the current event tag was to warn users that the article may not be current. If nobody is making new contributions then all the more reason to add it until an appropriate amount of time has passed, then revisit as a draft, stub, or merge. [[User:AllPurposeScientist|'''AllPurposeScientist''']][[User_talk:AllPurposeScientist|blah]] 18:03, 31 August 2024 (UTC) == Requested move 1 September 2024 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] after discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''moved.''' ([[Wikipedia:Requested moves/Closing instructions#Closure by a page mover|closed by non-admin page mover]]) [[User:Frost|'''''Frost''''']] 16:10, 8 September 2024 (UTC) ---- [[:2024 Israeli military operation in the northern West Bank]] → {{no redirect|2024 Israeli military operation in the West Bank}} – While the invasion of the West Bank was initially mainly in the North, it is has now spread to the entirety of the region. As @[[User:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|The Great Mule of Eupatoria]] map shows, and as various sources are beginning to report (https://www.timesofisrael.com/3-killed-in-southern-west-bank-shooting-attack-idf-pursuing-gunmen/) (https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/01/world/middleeast/israel-west-bank-police.html) the incursion is now also in Hebron in the south. These sources connect the latest attack to the wider invasion of the West Bank. [[User:Genabab|Genabab]] ([[User talk:Genabab|talk]]) 13:27, 1 September 2024 (UTC) :'''Support''' there has been recent activity in Hebron which is in the south, just today there was resistance targeting three Israeli security forces in the city and it has expanded operations there :https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/least-two-israelis-killed-west-bank-shooting-attack-medics-say-2024-09-01/ :my only suggestion is: should it be operation or invasion? I’m not sure if it’s large enough to be considered as the latter but it is larger scale than previous raids [[User:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|The Great Mule of Eupatoria]] ([[User talk:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|talk]]) 15:07, 1 September 2024 (UTC) :Obvious support but first, I think the revert by {{ping|Galamore}} of my move that removed "northern" was unwarranted; can you support your claim by providing evidence showing how RS titles have used "northern" first? [[User:Makeandtoss|Makeandtoss]] ([[User talk:Makeandtoss|talk]]) 13:43, 2 September 2024 (UTC) *'''Support''' Should be uncontroversial. The current title is [[WP:OVERPRECISE]] regardless, but as others have said, there is also fighting in the southern part of the West Bank. [[User:Gödel2200|Gödel2200]] ([[User talk:Gödel2200|talk]]) 15:09, 2 September 2024 (UTC) :'''Support''' as more concise and with "northern" being somewhat unnecessary. Moreover, operations have begun in the south now, so it's moved on. [[User:Iskandar323|Iskandar323]] ([[User talk:Iskandar323|talk]]) 18:29, 5 September 2024 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.
== what happened to the MAGAV members who were KIA? == It seems as though someone deleted the 3 Israeli occupation police officers who were killed in a driveby shooting in the occupied West Bank. Those should count towards Israeli military casualties as well. [[Special:Contributions/99.237.230.117|99.237.230.117]] ([[User talk:99.237.230.117|talk]]) 18:08, 23 September 2024 (UTC) :addendum-- I see the article on what I'm talking about, but for some reason the MAGAV who were killed are only described as "wounded". Wondering if someone can change the description to "KIA" instead to reflect the headline? [[Special:Contributions/99.237.230.117|99.237.230.117]] ([[User talk:99.237.230.117|talk]]) 18:37, 23 September 2024 (UTC) == Allenby Bridge shooting == Are the three who were shot by the Jordanian national at Allenby Bridge not officially employees of the israeli security apparatus? As in uniformed border personnel? [[Special:Contributions/99.237.230.117|99.237.230.117]] ([[User talk:99.237.230.117|talk]]) 18:10, 23 September 2024 (UTC) == Requested move 24 September 2024 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] after discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''no consensus.''' ([[Wikipedia:Requested moves/Closing instructions#Non-admin closure|non-admin closure]]) [[User:Alpha3031|Alpha3031]] ([[User talk:Alpha3031|t]] • [[Special:Contributions/Alpha3031|c]]) 07:06, 20 October 2024 (UTC) ---- [[:2024 Israeli military operation in the West Bank]] → {{no redirect|Operation Summer Camps}} – While the operation has been given two names (by the two sides) "Operation Summer Camps" appears to be the name most widely used in sources. (Simply look up "Operation Summer Camps" vs "Horror of the Camps operation" in Google news to see what I mean.) Additionally, using the Israeli name of the operation is also in parallel to [[Operation Defensive Shield]], which is, interestingly, the other big large-scale West Bank operation to which this ongoing one has been compared to. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 04:51, 24 September 2024 (UTC) — '''''Relisting.''''' [[User: Arnav Bhate|Arnav Bhate]] ([[User talk:Arnav Bhate|talk]] • [[Special:Contributions/Arnav Bhate|contribs]]) 17:27, 2 October 2024 (UTC) — '''''Relisting.''''' [[User:Extraordinary Writ|Extraordinary Writ]] ([[User talk:Extraordinary Writ|talk]]) 08:36, 10 October 2024 (UTC) :'''Support.''' The current title, in my opinion, is too broad given how many military operations Israel has regularly launched into the West Bank in 2024. In addition, Wikipedia has had no problem referring to Israeli military operations in past wars by their Israeli name, such as with [[Operation Defensive Shield]], so I don’t see a reason why this war’s naming process should be different. [[User:SpiderMandem|SpiderMandem]] ([[User talk:SpiderMandem|talk]]) 16:41, 17 October 2024 (UTC) (I am not ''strongly'' in favor of such a potential move- it is more so a recommendation based on observations.) [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 04:51, 24 September 2024 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' Makes it clearer what the article is about, and "operation summer camps" make it sound something nice, which many would question. [[User:PatGallacher|PatGallacher]] ([[User talk:PatGallacher|talk]]) 12:48, 11 October 2024 (UTC) *:To be fair, it doesn't really matter what vibe the name gives off, it's simply the name of the operation [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 20:52, 11 October 2024 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' Nowadays we refrain from using names given by the sides in favor of descriptive titles. I see no reason to depart from that practice. [[User:Selfstudier|Selfstudier]] ([[User talk:Selfstudier|talk]]) 17:08, 17 October 2024 (UTC) *'''Oppose''' per the points already raised, and also simply because for the average reader the proposed title means nothing. It's good enough for an AKA in the lead but nothing more. - [[User:Ïvana|Ïvana]] ([[User talk:Ïvana|talk]]) 01:08, 19 October 2024 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.
== Infobox casualties == We should either put both sides' civilian casualties in the bottom section, or we should both sides' civilian casualties alongside the military ones. I support the latter option. But currently, we have Israeli civilian casualties alongside the military ones, while Palestinian military casualties separated.'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' (Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply) 06:29, 25 September 2024 (UTC) == The conclusion is for that specific raid in tulkarm, not the entire op == The "idf announces end of operation on november 7th" is misleading as summer camps is ongoing, but that specific raid in tulkarm is over for now, [[Special:Contributions/108.29.64.228|108.29.64.228]] ([[User talk:108.29.64.228|talk]]) 22:39, 9 November 2024 (UTC) == Requested move 6 March 2025 ==
:''The following is a closed discussion of a [[Wikipedia:Requested moves|requested move]]. '''Please do not modify it.''' Subsequent comments should be made in a new section on the talk page. Editors desiring to contest the closing decision should consider a [[Wikipedia:move review|move review]] '''after''' discussing it on the closer's talk page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.'' The result of the move request was: '''No consensus'''. Has been open for a long time, and no support other than the nom, with previous RMs also not finding consensus.  — [[User:Amakuru|Amakuru]] ([[User talk:Amakuru|talk]]) 14:56, 25 March 2025 (UTC) ---- [[:2024 Israeli military operation in the West Bank]] → {{no redirect|Operation Summer Camps}} – Most articles over at [[List of Israel Defense Forces operations]] use the operation names as the page title instead of a very lengthy descriptive title. [[User:Evaporation123|Evaporation123]] ([[User talk:Evaporation123|talk]]) 22:51, 4 March 2025 (UTC) ''This is a contested technical request'' ([[Special:Permalink/1279100293#J4Z|permalink]]). [[User:CFA|C]] F [[User talk:CFA|A]] 15:12, 6 March 2025 (UTC) **Contesting: there have been multiple RMs on the talk page, so this isn't uncontroversial. [[User:Extraordinary Writ|Extraordinary Writ]] ([[User talk:Extraordinary Writ|talk]]) 08:19, 5 March 2025 (UTC) **:'''Oppose'''. No evidence of [[WP:COMMONNAME]] has been provided and the current title is more [[WP:RECOGNIZABLE]]. Also, the listings at [[List of Israel Defense Forces operations]] don't reflect article titles. For example the "Operation Shevet Ahim" listing points to "Israeli disengagement from the Gaza Strip".'''[[User talk:Vice regent|VR]]''' (Please [[Template:Ping|ping]] on reply) 21:24, 18 March 2025 (UTC)
The discussion above is closed. Please do not modify it. Subsequent comments should be made on the appropriate discussion page. No further edits should be made to this discussion.

sections.line: "The Israeli operation came about following a failed suicide bombing in Tel Aviv by a Hamas operative, and calls by Hamas for the renewel of suicide attacks."
what happened to the MAGAV members who were KIA?
The conclusion is for that specific raid in tulkarm, not the entire op
